reasoning_content:
We need to determine if 22717 is a prime number. Let's check for divisibility by small primes.

First, note that 22717 is odd, so not divisible by 2. Sum of digits: 2+2+7+1+7=19, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Last digit is 7, not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check divisibility by 7: 7*3245=22715, remainder 2, so not divisible by 7. Check 11: alternating sum: 2-2+7-1+7=13, not divisible by 11. Check 13: 13*1747=22711, remainder 6, not divisible. 17: 17*1336=22712, remainder 5; 17*1337=22729 too high. 19: 19*1195=22705, remainder 12; 19*1196=22724 too high. 23: 23*988=22724, too high? Actually 23*987=22701, remainder 16; 23*988=22724, so no. 29: 29*783=22707, remainder 10; 29*784=22736 too high. 31: 31*733=22723, remainder -6; 31*732=22692, remainder 25; not divisible. 37: 37*614=22718, too high? Actually 37*614=22718, so remainder -1; 37*613=22681, remainder 36; no. 41: 41*554=22714, remainder 3; 41*555=22755 too high. 43: 43*528=22704, remainder 13; 43*529=22747 too high. 47: 47*483=22701, remainder 16; 47*484=22748 too high. 53: 53*428=22684, remainder 33; 53*429=22737 too high. 59: 59*385=22715, remainder 2; 59*386=22774 too high. 61: 61*372=22692, remainder 25; 61*373=22753 too high. 67: 67*339=22713, remainder 4; 67*340=22780 too high. 71: 71*320=22720, remainder -3; 71*319=22649, remainder 68; no. 73: 73*311=22703, remainder 14; 73*312=22776 too high. 79: 79*287=22673, remainder 44; 79*288=22752 too high. 83: 83*273=22659, remainder 58; 83*274=22742 too high. 89: 89*255=22695, remainder 22; 89*256=22784 too high. 97: 97*234=22698, remainder 19; 97*235=22795 too high. 101: 101*225=22725, remainder -8; 101*224=22624, remainder 93; no.

We need to check up to sqrt(22717). sqrt(22717) ≈ 150.72 (since 150^2=22500, 151^2=22801). So we need to test primes up to 151. So far we've tested up to 97. Continue:

103: 103*220=22660, remainder 57; 103*221=22763 too high. 107: 107*212=22684, remainder 33; 107*213=22791 too high. 109: 109*208=22672, remainder 45; 109*209=22781 too high. 113: 113*201=22713, remainder 4; 113*202=22826 too high. 127: 127*179=22733, remainder -16; 127*178=22606, remainder 111; so no. 131: 131*173=22663, remainder 54; 131*174=22794 too high. 137: 137*165=22605, remainder 112; 137*166=22742 too high. 139: 139*163=22657, remainder 60; 139*164=22796 too high. 149: 149*152=22648, remainder 69; 149*153=22797 too high. 151: 151*150=22650, remainder 67; 151*151=22801 > 22717.

So no divisor found. Therefore, 22717 is prime. But we should double-check if any prime we missed, like 17? Already did. Also check 19, 23, etc. It seems none divide.

Thus answer: yes.
